ocr.text: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of patients diagnosed with R. equi infections, highlighting
critical aspects of their clinical profiles and treatment outcomes. A notable aspect of the data is the onset of symptoms, which
indicates that the majority of patients presented with common respiratory symptoms such as fever, cough, and dyspnea, with the
earliest recorded onset in May 2005.
Burden  Yes  No  Not mentioned
Had to apply for a loan  45 (15.4)  209 (71.3)  16 (13.3)
Had to sell a property  23 (7.8)  222 (75.8)  25 (16.4)
Had a potential income loss  56 (19.1)  192 (65.5)  22 (15.4)
Had to restricted other expenses  99 (33.8)  154 (52.6)  17 (13.6)
Onset of symptoms (date)  Presenting symptoms  Site of R. equi infection (diagnostic technique)  CD 4 + cells, HIV-1 RNA (VL)  HAART  Treatment  Duration

May 2005  fever, cough, dyspnea  lung (culture of sputum, chest X-ray, chest high resolution CT scan)  133 cells/μl, VL: 40,700 copies/ml  lamivudine, tenofovir, efavirenz  induction (i.v.): rifampicin 600 mg qd, levofloxacin 500 mg qd, azythromicin 500 mg qd maintenance (p.o.): levofloxacin 500 mg qd azytromicin 500 mg qd  induction: 2 weeks maintenance: 8 weeks

September 2009  weight loss, fever, subcutaneous nodule in the right thigh  subcutaneous thigh tissue (culture of granuloma, MRI, needle aspirate); colon (colonoscopy with biopsy)  90 cells/μl VL: 78,650 copies/ml  emtricitabine, tenofovir, atazanavir  azythromicin i.v. 500 mg qd, levofloxacin i.v. 750 mg qd, ryfampicin i.v. 600 mg qd, switched to rifabutin p.o. 150 mg q48h at beginning of HAART  8 weeks

November 2009  fever, ascites, diarrhoea  colon, peritoneum, abdominal lymph nodes, lung (total body CT and PET scan)  59 cells/μl, VL: 778 copies/ml  emtricitabine, tenofovir, darunavir/r  induction (i.v.): imipenem 1 g q6h amikacin 1 g qd maintenance (p.o.): azythromicin 500 mg qd, levofloxacin 750 mg qd  induction: 3 weeks maintenance: 10 weeks

March 2010  fever, abdominal pain and intestinal bleeding  disseminated infection (blood culture); colon (CT scan and histological examination of surgically removed colonic tissue); lung (biopsy); subcutaneous thigh tissue (culture of needle aspirate, MRI); skin.  62 cells/μl VL < 50 copies/ml  emtricitabine, tenofovir, darunavir/r  surgery induction (i.v.): imipenem 500 mg q6h, switched to ertapenem 1 g qd after 7 weeks, levofloxacin 750 mg qd, vancomicin 500 mg bid, azythromicin 500 mg qd maintenance (p.o.): azythromicin 500 mg qd, rifabutin 150 mg q48h  induction: 14 weeks maintenance: 8 weeks

August 2010  headache  CNS (brain CT scan and MRI)  74 cells/μl VL < 50 copies/ml  emtricitabine, tenofovir, darunavir/r  induction (i.v.): imipenem 500 mg q6h, amikacin 1 g qd maintenance (i.v.): meropenem 1 g q8h, azythromicin 500 mg qd, switched to ertapenem 1 g qd after 10 weeks  induction: 3 weeks maintenance: 10 weeks
Procedure  n  %
EUA/IOPM  327  38.3
NLD probe/ tube insertion  81  9.4
Suture removal  22  2.6
Other  95  11.1
Among the cohort, a significant proportion of patients, 209 (71.3%), experienced these symptoms, underscoring the urgent
nature of their medical condition. The table further elucidates the diagnostic techniques employed, revealing that lung infections
were primarily identified through sputum cultures, chest X-rays, and high-resolution CT scans. Additionally, the immunological
status of patients is reflected in the CD4+ cell counts, with an average of 133 cells/μl observed, alongside a viral load of 40,700
copies/ml of HIV-1 RNA. This information is crucial for understanding the interplay between HIV status and susceptibility to
R. equi infections, informing both treatment strategies and patient management protocols.
